titleEn: Agreenculture acquires Chouette to add agronomic intelligence to autonomous machines
titleZh: Agreenculture收购Chouette，为自主农机增添农艺智能
summaryZh: 法国自主机械专家Agreenculture收购了专注葡萄园AI农艺分析的公司Chouette，将自主驾驶技术与作物监测算法结合，打造能独立作业并解读田间信息的智能农机。Chouette的技术通过拖拉机传感器采集高分辨率图像，识别白粉病、葡萄藤黄化病等病害，并生成健康地图。双方计划将技术扩展至果园等特种作物，以应对劳动力短缺、成本上升等挑战。
featuredReason: 该新闻展示了AI与自主农机在特种作物（葡萄园）中的深度融合，为智慧畜牧行业（尤其是果园/种植园管理）提供了从“自动化”到“智能化”的实践范本，值得关注其技术路径和商业模式。
contentFull: Agreenculture acquires Chouette to add agronomic intelligence to autonomous machines 25-06 | Autonomous/semi-autosteering systems | NewsLinkedInFacebookXWhatsAppPrintChouette brings a different capability to that mix: onboard agronomic analysis that can translate field observations into crop management insights, including vine health monitoring, in-field variability mapping, and early disease detection. Photo: AgreencultureFrench autonomous-machinery specialist Agreenculture has acquired Chouette, a company specialising in AI-powered agronomic analysis for viticulture. The deal pairs Agreenculture’s autonomy technology with Chouette’s crop-monitoring algorithms, with the aim of building machines that can both operate independently and interpret what they observe in the field.The acquisition is part of a broader strategy by Agreenculture to build an integrated platform spanning both machine route management and parcel-level agronomic analysis. Combined with Chouette’s team, Agreenculture’s roughly 60-person industrial and technical workforce is expected to accelerate ongoing development work, particularly on detecting grapevine flavescence dorée, a serious phytosanitary threat to French viticulture.Extending underlying technologyThe companies plan to extend the underlying technology to other specialty crops, such as orchards, and to gradually fold these capabilities into the autonomous solutions Agreenculture offers equipment manufacturers. The overarching ambition, according to the companies, is to tackle some of the sector’s most pressing challenges: labour shortages, rising input costs, farm profitability, and the broader environmental transition facing agriculture.Autonomy kits Agreenculture has spent over a decade building autonomy kits that let equipment manufacturers deploy tractors and machines capable of running without local supervision while meeting European safety standards. Chouette brings a different capability to that mix: onboard agronomic analysis that can translate field observations into crop management insights, including vine health monitoring, in-field variability mapping, and early disease detection.A substantial datasetCEO Christophe Aubé of Agreenculture framed the acquisition as a missing piece for autonomous farming. According to Aubé, autonomous field operations are incomplete without genuine agronomic understanding of the terrain. Chouette’s technology relies on sensors mounted to tractors that capture high-resolution field imagery. The system processes those images to flag conditions such as powdery mildew, esca, and missing vines, while tracking canopy Vigor and generating georeferenced maps of vineyard health.The company has built up a substantial dataset in the process: more than 200 million images processed across over 64,000 hectares in France – roughly 8% of the country’s total vineyard area – spanning 11 growing seasons.Start of a new phaseChouette co-founders Cyril de Chassey and Charles Nespoulous describe the acquisition as the start of a new phase, noting that embedding their technology directly into Agreenculture’s products opens a new route into the fast-growing agricultural robotics market. Chouette agronomy product manager Marine Perrier said the combination works in both directions: Agreenculture’s GNSS precision and connectivity is expected to strengthen Chouette’s solutions, while Chouette’s agronomic algorithms broaden what Agreenculture can offer.LinkedInFacebookXWhatsAppPrint René Groeneveld Correspondent for AustraliaMore aboutagricultural robotsagronomistsautonomous technologycrop monitoringvineyard robotsadLEARN MORERelated articles15-07 | NewsNorwegian robotics firm AutoAgri rebrands as Uncrew Robotics14-07 | NewsSabanto secures oversubscribed funding round, betting on smaller platforms10-07 | NewsXAG launches RM80 unmanned mower, unveils new X Series drone system02-07 | NewsTersus GNSS AG993: Modular autosteer kit with built-in satellite correction fallback Buyers guides & Catalogues Subscribe to our YouTube channel Taiwan Smart Agriweek 2026Sep 8-10, 2026 TaiNEX 1, Tapei
translationZh:
法国自主机械专家Agreenculture已收购Chouette，一家专注于葡萄栽培领域AI驱动农艺分析的公司。这笔交易将Agreenculture的自主技术与Chouette的作物监测算法相结合，旨在打造既能独立作业又能解读田间所见信息的机器。

此次收购是Agreenculture更广泛战略的一部分，即构建一个涵盖机器路径管理和地块级农艺分析的综合平台。结合Chouette的团队，Agreenculture约60人的工业和技术人员队伍预计将加速正在进行的开发工作，特别是在检测葡萄藤黄化病方面——这是对法国葡萄栽培构成严重植物检疫威胁的一种病害。

**扩展底层技术**

两家公司计划将底层技术扩展到其他特种作物，如果园，并逐步将这些能力整合到Agreenculture向设备制造商提供的自主解决方案中。据两家公司称，总体目标是应对该行业一些最紧迫的挑战：劳动力短缺、投入成本上升、农场盈利能力以及农业面临的更广泛的环境转型。

**自主套件**

Agreenculture花了十多年时间构建自主套件，使设备制造商能够部署无需本地监督即可运行且符合欧洲安全标准的拖拉机和机器。Chouette为这一组合带来了不同的能力：车载农艺分析，可将田间观察转化为作物管理洞察，包括葡萄藤健康监测、田间变异绘图和早期病害检测。

**庞大的数据集**

Agreenculture首席执行官Christophe Aubé将此次收购定位为自主农业缺失的一环。据Aubé称，没有对地形的真正农艺理解，自主田间作业是不完整的。Chouette的技术依赖于安装在拖拉机上的传感器，这些传感器可捕获高分辨率田间图像。该系统处理这些图像以标记白粉病、葡萄藤枯梢病和缺株等状况，同时跟踪冠层活力并生成葡萄园健康的地理参考地图。

该公司在此过程中积累了庞大的数据集：在法国超过64,000公顷的土地上处理了超过2亿张图像——约占该国葡萄园总面积的8%——跨越11个生长季。

**新阶段的开始**

Chouette联合创始人Cyril de Chassey和Charles Nespoulous将此次收购描述为新阶段的开始。
author: René Groeneveld
contentHtml: French autonomous-machinery specialist Agreenculture has acquired Chouette, a company specialising in AI-powered agronomic analysis for viticulture. The deal pairs Agreenculture’s autonomy technology with Chouette’s crop-monitoring algorithms, with the aim of building machines that can both operate independently and interpret what they observe in the field.
